summary: The false statement is that lymphoid tissues grow to 200% by age 13 and regress afterward; this lacks support from sources showing they instead approach adult size around age 6 without such specific percentage or timing details. The other options correctly reflect known patterns—neural tissues nearing adulthood by six years and genital development completing during puberty.
